senses.definition: Pixels per inch; a measure of how many pixels appear in one inch of a digital image or screen, showing how sharp it is.
examples: This monitor has a resolution of 110 ppi, so the text looks very sharp.
Designers often choose images with a higher ppi for printing.
A phone screen with more ppi usually looks clearer.

senses.definition: Producer Price Index; an economic measure that shows how the prices that producers receive for their goods and services change over time.
examples: The latest PPI figures show a rise in factory prices this month.
Investors watch the PPI closely to predict inflation trends.
A falling PPI can signal lower inflation in the future.

senses.definition: Payment protection insurance; a type of insurance that covers loan or credit payments if you cannot pay because of illness, unemployment, or death.
examples: Many people complained that they had been mis-sold PPI by their banks.
You can claim a refund if your PPI was added without your consent.
The PPI scandal led to large compensation payments.

senses.definition: An informal written abbreviation for ‘people’, used especially in text messages and online.
examples: How many ppl are coming to the party?
Some ppl prefer to work from home.
I like meeting new ppl when I travel.

senses.definition: Parts per million; a unit for measuring how much of one substance is mixed into another substance.
examples: The level of lead in the water was 50 ppm, which is considered unsafe.
This device can detect gas concentrations as low as 5 ppm.
Engineers measured the CO2 in the air in ppm.

senses.definition: Post-postscript; an extra note that you add at the end of a letter or message after ‘PS’.
examples: PPS: I forgot to say, happy birthday!
She added a PPS at the bottom of the email with the meeting time.
PPS is sometimes used in informal letters for an extra comment.

senses.definition: A file or presentation created with Microsoft PowerPoint software.
examples: Please send me the ppt from your talk yesterday.
She prepared a ppt for her class presentation.
The trainer used a colorful ppt to explain the topic.

senses.definition: Public relations; the work of creating a good image for a person or organization in the eyes of the public.
examples: The company hired a pr firm to handle the crisis.
Good pr can help a brand recover after a mistake.
She works in pr for a large tech company.

senses.definition: A flat-bottomed boat used as a small working or fishing boat or as a helper boat for a larger ship.
examples: The fishermen loaded their nets into the narrow praam.
A small praam carried supplies out to the anchored ship.
They crossed the calm harbor in an old wooden praam.

senses.definition: Plural of praam; several flat-bottomed working or fishing boats.
examples: Praams lined the riverbank, waiting for the morning tide.
They hired two praams to move the cargo.
Old wooden praams lay pulled up on the beach.

senses.definition: A small argument or noisy disagreement.
examples: They had a little prabble over who should pay the bill.
The play is full of comic prabbles between neighbors.
After a brief prabble, they quickly made peace.

senses.definition: Plural of prabble; several small arguments or noisy disagreements.
examples: Their marriage was marked by frequent prabbles and reconciliations.
The novel describes the prabbles of village life.
He tried to stay out of the family prabbles.

senses.definition: A respectful title or name in South Asian languages meaning ‘lord’ or ‘master’, sometimes used for God or for respected men.
examples: In many prayers, devotees address God as Prabhu.
The teacher was fondly called Prabhu by his students.
Prabhu is a common male given name in India.

senses.definition: In India, a person who travels and works to spread a cause or organization’s ideas, often through meetings and public talks.
examples: He served as a pracharak, organizing meetings in rural areas.
Each pracharak is responsible for building local support.
The party sent several pracharaks to campaign in the region.

senses.definition: Plural of pracharak; people who work to spread a cause or organization’s ideas through personal contact and events.
examples: The organization relies on its pracharaks to reach remote villages.
Several pracharaks coordinated the campaign.
Local pracharaks arranged public lectures and meetings.

senses.definition: An old or very rare form of ‘practical’, meaning related to action or practice rather than ideas.
examples: The book focuses on practic skills rather than theory.
She valued practic experience over formal education.
His approach was strictly practic and results-driven.

senses.definition: Plural of practicability; different degrees or kinds of how possible or workable something is in real life.
examples: They discussed the various practicabilities of the proposal.
The report weighs the costs and practicabilities of several options.
Different practicabilities must be considered in changing environments.

senses.definition: The quality of being possible to do or use successfully in real life.
examples: We need to test the practicability of this plan before investing.
The committee questioned the practicability of the new regulations.
Cost is an important factor in the practicability of the project.

senses.definition: Possible to do or use successfully.
examples: This solution is not practicable within the current budget.
We will explore all practicable options before deciding.
The plan is technically practicable but politically difficult.

senses.definition: The state of being practicable or possible to carry out in real life.
examples: We must consider the practicableness of these recommendations.
The practicableness of the proposal remains uncertain.
He argued for the practicableness of the reform plan.

senses.definition: Plural of practicableness; different kinds or instances of being able to be done in practice.
examples: The various practicablenesses of the options were analyzed.
They debated the political and legal practicablenesses of the treaty.
Several practicablenesses and limitations were outlined in the report.

senses.definition: In a way that is practicable; as far as is possible in real conditions.
examples: We will support you as practicably as we can.
The rules should be applied as uniformly as practicably possible.
All practicably available measures were taken to reduce risk.

senses.definition: Connected with real situations and actions rather than ideas or theories.
examples: The course gives students practical skills for the workplace.
We need a practical solution to this problem.
She has a lot of practical experience in marketing.

senses.definition: Sensible and able to make good decisions based on what will work in real life.
examples: He is a very practical person who plans carefully.
Being practical, she saved money for emergencies.
Their practical nature helped them stay calm in a crisis.

senses.definition: A strong focus on practical results and everyday usefulness, rather than on ideas or theories.
examples: His philosophy of education is based on practicalism.
The culture of the organization leans toward strict practicalism.
Critics say that pure practicalism can ignore important values.

senses.definition: Plural of practicalism; different forms or examples of putting practical concerns above theory or ideals.
examples: The essay explores various practicalisms in modern politics.
Their policies show the practicalisms of daily governance.
Different cultural practicalisms affect how problems are solved.

senses.definition: A person who strongly prefers practical results and real-world solutions to theories or ideals.
examples: As a practicalist, she focused on what could actually be done.
The minister is a practicalist rather than a dreamer.
Practicalists often clash with more idealistic thinkers.

senses.definition: Plural of practicalist; people who prefer practical results and real-world solutions to theories or ideals.
examples: Practicalists and idealists often disagree about policy.
The committee is made up of experienced practicalists.
Many practicalists support gradual, workable reforms.

senses.definition: The real facts, details, and tasks that are part of a situation or plan, rather than ideas about it.
examples: We need to think about the practicalities of moving abroad.
The book deals with the practicalities of running a small business.
They discussed the practicalities of caring for an elderly parent.

senses.definition: The quality of being sensible and likely to work well in real life.
examples: We questioned the practicality of his suggestion.
Her plan has a certain practicality that appeals to managers.
The design scores high for both beauty and practicality.

senses.definition: The process of making something more practical, usable, or suitable for real-life use.
examples: The practicalization of the theory took many years of testing.
Funding is needed for the practicalization of these ideas.
They worked on the practicalization of new teaching methods.

senses.definition: To make something more practical or ready for use in real situations.
examples: The training program aims to practicalize theoretical knowledge.
They tried to practicalize the abstract model for everyday use.
Her job is to practicalize new ideas within the company.

senses.definition: Past form of ‘practicalize’: made more practical or ready for real use.
examples: The theory was practicalized through years of fieldwork.
These guidelines were practicalized in several pilot projects.
Once practicalized, the method spread quickly.

senses.definition: A person who makes ideas or plans practical and usable.
examples: He describes himself as a practicalizer of new technologies.
The team needs both creative thinkers and practicalizers.
As a practicalizer, she turns concepts into working products.

senses.definition: In a practical way, focusing on real situations and results.
examples: We need to think practically about our chances of success.
She dealt with the problem very practically.
Practically speaking, we cannot finish this today.

senses.definition: Almost; nearly.
examples: The two machines are practically the same.
I’ve read practically all of his books.
It was practically impossible to hear him.

senses.definition: The quality of being practical, sensible, and useful in real life.
examples: We admired the practicalness of her design.
His practicalness helped the project stay on track.
The car’s main strength is its practicalness.

senses.definition: Plural of practicalness; different ways or degrees in which something is practical.
examples: They compared the practicalnesses of several designs.
The report lists the strengths and practicalnesses of each option.
Different practicalnesses matter in different contexts.

senses.definition: Classes or activities, especially in science or technical subjects, where students do hands-on work rather than only listening to theory.
examples: The course includes weekly practicals in the lab.
Students must pass both the exam and the practicals.
During the practicals, they learned how to use the equipment safely.

senses.definition: A person who is practising or training in a profession, especially in older or non-standard use.
examples: She worked as a practicant in a legal office.
The hospital accepted several practicants each year.
As a young practicant, he learned from senior doctors.

senses.definition: The act of doing an activity again and again to improve your skill.
examples: Your English will improve with regular practice.
She plays the piano every day for practice.
With more practice, you will feel more confident.

senses.definition: The usual way of doing something in a particular place or situation.
examples: It is common practice to check all equipment before use.
Company practice varies from country to country.
The new system changes long-standing practice.

senses.definition: The work or business of a professional person such as a doctor or lawyer.
examples: She runs a small medical practice in the city.
He joined a law practice after graduation.
The practice has three partners and ten staff.

senses.definition: To do an activity many times so that you become better at it.
examples: You need to practice speaking English every day.
She practices the violin for two hours a day.
He is practicing for the driving test.

senses.definition: Experienced and skilled because you have done something many times.
examples: With a practiced hand, she tied the bandage quickly.
He gave a practiced smile to the cameras.
The dancer moved with practiced ease.

senses.definition: Past form of ‘practice’: did an activity many times to improve your ability.
examples: She practiced her presentation until she felt ready.
They practiced together every weekend.
I practiced writing essays during the course.

senses.definition: The quality of being very experienced and skilled through much practice.
examples: There was a clear practicedness in her movements.
His practicedness showed in how calmly he handled the crisis.
The practicedness of the team impressed everyone.

senses.definition: Someone who regularly does an activity in order to improve at it.
examples: She is a serious practicer of yoga.
Good musicians are constant practicers.
As a regular practicer, he quickly improved his skills.

senses.definition: Plural of practicer; people who regularly do an activity to improve at it.
examples: The best athletes are dedicated practicers.
Language practicers often meet in conversation clubs.
Serious practicers make time every day to train.

senses.definition: Plural of practice; repeated actions to improve skill.
examples: Her daily practices have made her a better singer.
Regular writing practices help students gain confidence.
The team’s practices are held three times a week.

senses.definition: The usual ways of doing things in different fields or places.
examples: Teaching practices differ from country to country.
Good business practices include honest communication.
These practices are now common in many schools.

senses.definition: Third person singular of ‘practice’: does an activity many times to get better.
examples: She practices the guitar after school.
He practices speaking English with his friends.
The team practices twice a week.

senses.definition: A person who is skilled through practical experience, especially a professional such as a doctor.
examples: An experienced practician quickly recognized the symptoms.
The book was written by a practician with many years in the field.
Practicians often develop their own methods over time.

senses.definition: An attitude or system that strongly values practical experience and skilled practice.
examples: The school’s culture reflects a kind of practicianism.
Critics argue that extreme practicianism may ignore theory.
His essay defends practicianism in professional education.

senses.definition: Plural of practician; people who are skilled through practical work, often in a profession.
examples: The conference brought together practicians from many countries.
Young practicians benefit from mentoring.
Practicians and researchers shared their views on the topic.

senses.definition: Continuous form of ‘practice’: doing an activity again and again to get better.
examples: She is practicing her speech in front of the mirror.
They were practicing for the school concert.
I’m practicing writing essays for the exam.

senses.definition: Actively working in a particular job or profession.
examples: She is a practicing lawyer in New York.
The course is designed for practicing teachers.
As a practicing doctor, he has many real-life stories.

senses.definition: An old or dialect spelling of ‘practice’, especially meaning a scheme or trick.
examples: The story tells of an evil practick against the king.
In old texts, practick sometimes refers to magic or plots.
He warned them of the practicks of dishonest traders.
